preview_text:
The statistical model favors Morocco by a narrow margin, assigning them a 35% win probability against the Netherlands' 33%. The gap is marginal, reflecting a tightly matched contest in which either side holds a realistic claim to victory.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 32%, suggesting the teams are closely competitive across the win-loss spectrum. The spread across all three outcomes is relatively even, indicating high uncertainty and a match likely to hinge on fine margins rather than a pronounced imbalance in underlying strength.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices diverge notably from the model. Both betting sources favor the Netherlands substantially, crediting them an 11-percentage-point advantage over Morocco's chances -- a meaningful gap that the model does not detect. The draw estimate aligns across sources at roughly 30%. The dashboard offers no judgment on which assessment is correct; users should weigh the statistical model against market pricing according to their own confidence in each.
